tax: Placed at genus rank, Tumblestone is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a self-binding rubble sphere, not a welded solid mass. A new species belongs here only if it shows a rolling rubble body with a downhill attack run.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a rough sphere that flattens at rest. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — a swept central groove and a rubble fan — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Tumblestone occupies steep talus chutes. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where pressure binds loaded talus mid-slip and recur with a fresh rockfall reloads the chute, sustaining themselves on gravity.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: settles as a gravel mound, releases downhill. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: momentum mass and vibration-sense. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each species and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: acceleration and dust-plume. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Tumblestone matters because turns every steep chute into a one-way trap. It lets survey reports sort rolling hazards from walking ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Tumblestone is the genus-rank grouping of rolling rubble-sphere elementals within the True Elementals. Its members share a spherical body of bound talus that hunts by downhill momentum, and divide below into species groups by chute steepness and rubble grade. Fast, downhill-only, and helpless on the flat.
